assignment

2026-05-08

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(GenomicRanges)
  library(epiwraps)
  library(ggplot2)
  library(rGREAT) 
})
## Warning: package 'S4Vectors' was built under R version 4.5.3
options(timeout = 6000)
download.file("https://ethz-ins.org/content/clustering_assignment.zip", "clustering_assignment.zip")
unzip("clustering_assignment.zip")
list.files()
tracks <- list.files(pattern = "bw$", full.names = TRUE)
names(tracks) <- gsub("\\.bw", "", basename(tracks))
peakfiles <- list.files(pattern = "bed$", full.names = TRUE)
names(peakfiles) <- gsub("\\.bed", "", basename(peakfiles))
peaks <- lapply(peakfiles, rtracklayer::import.bed)
peaks <- list.files(pattern="bed$")
peaks <- lapply(peaks, rtracklayer::import.bed)
peaks <- lapply(peaks, FUN=function(x) x[x$score>800])
regions <- reduce(unlist(GRangesList(peaks)))

#Visualize relationship between binding of different proteins

# smaller subset to avoid crashing
set.seed(123)
regions_small <- sample(regions, min(1000, length(regions)))

ese_small <- signal2Matrix(tracks,regions_small,extend = 1000,w = 50)
## Reading ./Creb1.bw
## Reading ./Creb3.bw
## Reading ./Creb3L1.bw
plotEnrichedHeatmaps(ese_small, cluster_rows = TRUE, show_row_dend=TRUE )

Clustering

set.seed(123) 
cl <- clusterSignalMatrices(ese_small, k=4)
##   ~71% of the variance explained by clusters
table(cl)
## cl
##   1   2   3   4 
## 114 447 251 188
rowData(ese_small)$cluster <- cl
head(rowData(ese_small))
## DataFrame with 6 rows and 1 column
##                           cluster
##                          <factor>
## chr1:245791701-245792076        2
## chr1:28648517-28649031          1
## chr1:11841233-11841608          4
## chr1:205678570-205678945        2
## chr1:109075887-109076446        3
## chr1:117612537-117612989        2

#Plot clustered heatmap

plotEnrichedHeatmaps(ese_small, row_split=rowData(ese_small)$cluster, colors=c("white","darkred"),row_title= "Clusters of CREB-family binding regions")

#Plot average signal per cluster

d <- meltSignals(ese_small, splitBy = cl)

ggplot(d, aes(position, mean, colour = sample)) +geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) +facet_wrap(~split) +
  labs(title = "Average CREB-family signal by cluster",x = "Position relative to region center",y = "Mean signal", colour = "Track")

#Different number of clusters check

cl2 <- clusterSignalMatrices(ese_small, k=2:10)
ggplot(cl2$varExplained, aes(k, varExplained)) + geom_line()

  labs(title = "Variance explained by number of clusters",x = "Number of clusters",y = "Variance explained")
## <ggplot2::labels> List of 3
##  $ x    : chr "Number of clusters"
##  $ y    : chr "Variance explained"
##  $ title: chr "Variance explained by number of clusters"

#Enrichment analysis

split_regions <- split(rowRanges(ese_small), rowData(ese_small)$cluster)
lengths(split_regions)
##   1   2   3   4 
## 114 447 251 188
res <- great(split_regions[["2"]], gene_sets="GO:BP", tss_source="hg38", 
             background=regions, cores=2)
## * TSS source: TxDb.
## * check whether TxDb package 'TxDb.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38.knownGene' is installed.
## * gene ID type in the extended TSS is 'Entrez Gene ID'.
## * restrict chromosomes to 'chr1, chr2, chr3, chr4, chr5, chr6, chr7, chr8, chr9, chr10,
##     chr11, chr12, chr13, chr14, chr15, chr16, chr17, chr18, chr19, chr20, chr21, chr22, chrX,
##     chrY, chrM'.
## * 18193/35161 protein-coding genes left.
## * update seqinfo to the selected chromosomes.
## * TSS extension mode is 'basalPlusExt'.
## * construct the basal domains by extending 5000bp to upstream and 1000bp to downsteram of TSS.
## * calculate distances to neighbour regions.
## * extend to both sides until reaching the neighbour genes or to the maximal extension.
## * use GO:BP ontology with 15046 gene sets (source: org.Hs.eg.db).
## * check gene ID type in `gene_sets` and in `extended_tss`.
## * use self-defined background regions.
## * remove excluded regions from background.
## * overlap `gr` to background regions (based on midpoint).
## * in total 447 `gr`.
## * overlap extended TSS to background regions.
## * check which genes are in the gene sets.
## * only take gene sets with size >= 5.
## * in total 2479 gene sets.
## * overlap `gr` to every extended TSS.
## * perform binomial test for each biological term.
bp <- getEnrichmentTables(res)
head(bp)
##           id                      description genome_fraction
## 1 GO:1903522  regulation of blood circulation      0.02637693
## 2 GO:0008016  regulation of heart contraction      0.02401531
## 3 GO:0008015                blood circulation      0.04834704
## 4 GO:0003015                    heart process      0.02604362
## 5 GO:0060047                heart contraction      0.02604362
## 6 GO:0065008 regulation of biological quality      0.24884763
##   observed_region_hits fold_enrichment      p_value  p_adjust mean_tss_dist
## 1                   25        2.120353 0.0004407061 0.2894625         96343
## 2                   23        2.142556 0.0006426991 0.2894625        104123
## 3                   38        1.758353 0.0006477357 0.2894625         99195
## 4                   24        2.061590 0.0008356307 0.2894625        100931
## 5                   24        2.061590 0.0008356307 0.2894625        100931
## 6                  140        1.258598 0.0012497906 0.3207872         77034
##   observed_gene_hits gene_set_size fold_enrichment_hyper p_value_hyper
## 1                 13            20              1.672119   0.015583938
## 2                 11            17              1.664553   0.027154157
## 3                 22            35              1.616994   0.003134754
## 4                 12            19              1.624731   0.027059684
## 5                 12            19              1.624731   0.027059684
## 6                102           217              1.209189   0.004902005
##   p_adjust_hyper
## 1      0.1993833
## 2      0.2612833
## 3      0.1061651
## 4      0.2612833
## 5      0.2612833
## 6      0.1277317

#Plot top Biological Processes:

ggplot(head(bp,15), aes(fold_enrichment, reorder(description, p_adjust), 
                        size=observed_region_hits, color=-log10(p_adjust))) + 
  geom_point() + scale_color_viridis_c()

  labs(title = "GO Biological Process enrichment for cluster 1",x = "Fold enrichment",y = "Biological process",size = "Region hits",color = "-log10 adjusted p-value")
## <ggplot2::labels> List of 5
##  $ x     : chr "Fold enrichment"
##  $ y     : chr "Biological process"
##  $ size  : chr "Region hits"
##  $ colour: chr "-log10 adjusted p-value"
##  $ title : chr "GO Biological Process enrichment for cluster 1"

#Interpretation

print("The clustered heatmap and average signal profiles showed that some regions were strongly co-bound by Creb1 and Creb3L1, but other regions showed a more
selective enrichment for Creb3. Using k = 4 clusters explained approximately 71% of the variance while keeping interpretable binding patterns. This might show that members of the CREB family share binding at certain regulatory regions but also have protein-specific occupancy at others. GO enrichment analysis of cluster 2 showed strong localized binding and was associated with GO terms related to heart contraction, blood circulation, muscle contraction, and circulatory system processes. Even though adjusted p-values were modest after multiple-testing correction, this enrichment results show that this subset of CREB-family binding regions might be linked to cardiovascular and muscle-related regulatory functions.")
## [1] "The clustered heatmap and average signal profiles showed that some regions were strongly co-bound by Creb1 and Creb3L1, but other regions showed a more\nselective enrichment for Creb3. Using k = 4 clusters explained approximately 71% of the variance while keeping interpretable binding patterns. This might show that members of the CREB family share binding at certain regulatory regions but also have protein-specific occupancy at others. GO enrichment analysis of cluster 2 showed strong localized binding and was associated with GO terms related to heart contraction, blood circulation, muscle contraction, and circulatory system processes. Even though adjusted p-values were modest after multiple-testing correction, this enrichment results show that this subset of CREB-family binding regions might be linked to cardiovascular and muscle-related regulatory functions."
